message:
fix(search): apply tag filter before limit in extension search results

collect_search_hits was passing the user-requested limit directly to
index.search(), then filtering by tag afterward. This meant that if the
first N results were all wrong-tag, the function would return zero
results even though matching entries existed further down the list.

The fix searches the full index first (index.entries.len()), applies tag
and sort filters, then truncates to the requested limit. Also adds an
early return for limit==0 to avoid unnecessary work.

Includes a regression test (collect_search_hits_filters_by_tag_before_limit)
that constructs a two-entry index with different tags and verifies that
a limit-1 search with a tag filter returns the correct entry regardless
of its position in the index.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
